Within 21 days from the day of declaration of result / 21 days after the declaration
Within 21 days after the day of declaration of result
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An election petition must be filed within 21 days after the date of the declaration of the election result. Sec. 285(5), CFRN.

quizzes.scenario:
Indaboski Bahosa Senate election petition. Judgment timeframe:

The judgment of the Tribunal must be delivered within what time frame?
options: 180 days of declaration of election result
90 days of declaration of election result
120 days of filing election petition
180 days of filing election petition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: An election tribunal shall deliver its judgment in writing within 180 days from the date of filing the petition. Sec. 285(6), CFRN.

quizzes.scenario:
MATRIMONIAL PROCEEDINGS

Otunba J. F. Banjo and Miss Wuraola Jakande met at their NYSC camp and had been cohabiting together for more than 10 years, until 2015 when Otunba decided to marry one Sisi Alawiye at the Marriage Registry, Ibadan.

Only one of the following is true of the marriage between Otunba J. F. Banjo and Sisi Alawiye:
options: The marriage is invalid because of the existing cohabitation between Otunba and Miss Wuraola Jakande
The marriage is void as it was not celebrated in a licensed place of worship
The marriage is voidable at the option of Miss Wuraola
The marriage is valid
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The marriage is valid. Cohabitation, even for 10 years, does not create a statutory marriage in Nigeria. Therefore, Otunba is single and free to contract a valid statutory marriage with Sisi Alawiye. (Option D)

quizzes.scenario:
Otunba J. F. Banjo v Sisi Alawiye. Paternity issue:

Assuming the marriage was celebrated in a licensed place of worship and all other formalities were complied with. But two months after the wedding, Otunba J. F. Banjo discovered Sisi Alawiye was already 4 months pregnant, and she confessed that it was for her ex-boyfriend, Fiwajomi. The marriage from this fact is best described as:
options: Illegal marriage
Voidable marriage
Void marriage
Special status marriage
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 5(1)(d) of the Matrimonial Causes Act (MCA), a marriage is voidable if, at the time of the marriage, the respondent is pregnant by a person other than the petitioner.

quizzes.scenario:
Otunba J. F. Banjo v Sisi Alawiye. Remedy:

If Otunba J. F. Banjo intends to file a petition against Sisi Alawiye regarding the pregnancy, the most probable matrimonial relief he will be seeking is:
options: Dissolution of marriage
Nullity of a void marriage
Declaration of the marriage to be illegal
Nullity of a voidable marriage
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Since the marriage is voidable, the appropriate decree to seek is a decree of nullity of a voidable marriage.

quizzes.scenario:
FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS ENFORCEMENT

Dare Dude while traveling from Ibadan to Lagos in a commercial car was stopped by the Special Anti Robbery Squad (SARS) and alleged to be a fraudster as he was operating on his PC. His attempt to explain was met with a resounding round of slaps. He was taken into custody and has been in detention for more than 10 days without any access to his family members. His sister wants to bring an application for the enforcement of his fundamental rights.

The appropriate court with jurisdiction here is:
options: National Industrial Court
Oyo State High Court / High Court of Lagos State
Federal High Court / State High Court concurrently
Supreme Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In fundamental rights enforcements, both the Federal High Court and the States High Courts have concurrent jurisdiction. FUTMINNA v Olutayo.

quizzes.scenario:
Dare Dude fundamental rights enforcement action:

The action can be commenced through:
options: Writ of summons
Petition
Originating summons / Originating motion
Any of the above
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Fundamental Rights (Enforcement Procedure) Rules 2009, an application for the enforcement of fundamental rights may be commenced by any originating process, but in practice, an originating motion or originating summons is used.

quizzes.scenario:
Dare Dude fundamental rights enforcement action:

The following except one are documents to accompany the originating process (originating motion):
options: Statement of facts setting out the names, description, grounds and reliefs
Affidavits in support
Acknowledgement of petition
Written address in support of the application
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The required accompanying documents under the FREP Rules 2009 are: (1) Statement setting out the name/description, grounds, and reliefs; (2) Affidavit in support; and (3) Written address. Acknowledgement of petition is not required.

quizzes.scenario: Tunde and Eric wish to incorporate a logistics company in Lagos. They are informed that they must primarily deal with a specific agency for registration. Which body oversees the incorporation of all companies in Nigeria?
options: Securities and Exchange Commission
Corporate Affairs Commission
Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
Federal Inland Revenue Service
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) is the chief regulator established under Section 1 of CAMA 2020, responsible for the registration, regulation, and supervision of the formation and incorporation of companies. [1-3]

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner with 5 years of post-call experience and 2 years of corporate law practice wants to be appointed as the Registrar-General of the CAC. Is he qualified?
options: Yes, he is a legal practitioner
No, he needs 10 years post-call and 10 years corporate experience
No, he needs at least 10 years post-call and 8 years of corporate law experience
Yes, if the Minister recommends him
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 9 of CAMA 2020, the Registrar-General must be a legal practitioner qualified for at least 10 years and must have at least 8 years of corporate law experience. [4]

quizzes.scenario: XYZ Ltd intends to sue the Corporate Affairs Commission for a perceived wrong. The company's solicitor files the suit today without prior communication with the CAC. What is the status of this suit?
options: The suit is validly filed
The suit is incompetent for failure to give 30 days' pre-action notice
The suit should be filed at the State High Court
The suit requires the Attorney General's consent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 17(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that no suit shall be commenced against the Commission until the expiration of 30 days after a written notice of intention to sue has been served. [4, 5]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises regarding the removal of a director in a private company. The aggrieved director wants to file an action. Which court has exclusive jurisdiction?
options: State High Court
National Industrial Court
Federal High Court
Investment and Securities Tribunal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 251(1)(e) of the 1999 Constitution and Section 851(12) of CAMA 2020, the Federal High Court has exclusive jurisdiction over matters arising from the operation of CAMA. [6-8]

quizzes.scenario: A public company, ABC Plc, wants to offer its shares to the general public for the first time. Which agency is the apex regulator for this capital market operation?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission
Central Bank of Nigeria
Securities and Exchange Commission
Nigerian Exchange Limited
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 1 of the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) establishes the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) as the apex body overseeing capital market operations and the public offer of securities. [9-11]

quizzes.scenario: GlobalTech Inc., a US-based company, wants to open a branch in Nigeria to process cassava. Before they can legally begin operations as a foreign investor, they must register with which body?
options: Nigerian Export Promotion Council
Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion
Nigerian Immigration Service
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20 of the NIPC Act, foreign companies intending to operate in Nigeria must register with the Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC). [12-15]

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian company, TechHub Ltd, enters into a contract with a German firm to import a specialized software engine. Within what timeframe must they register this technology transfer contract?
options: 30 days
60 days
90 days
15 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: By Section 5(2) of the NOTAP Act, any contract for the transfer of foreign technology must be registered with the National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion (NOTAP) within 60 days of execution. [16-18]

quizzes.scenario: SecureGuard Ltd is a new company providing private security guards. Apart from CAMA, which specific Act must they comply with for licensing?
options: Police Act
Private Guard Companies Act
Labour Act
Criminal Code Act
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Private security firms are regulated by the Private Guard Companies Act in addition to general corporate regulations under CAMA. [19, 20]

quizzes.scenario: A pharmaceutical company, MedChem Ltd, wants to distribute a new cough syrup in Nigeria. Which agency's approval is mandatory for the safety and quality control of this product?
options: SON
FCCPC
NAFDAC
NIS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) is responsible for regulating the manufacture, distribution, and sale of drugs and food under the NAFDAC Act. [21-23]

quizzes.scenario: PowerSource Ltd wants to spend ₦750,000 on new heavy machinery for its factory. Which division must they notify regarding this capital expenditure?
options: Federal Inland Revenue Service
Industrial Inspectorate Division
Corporate Affairs Commission
Securities and Exchange Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Industrial Inspectorate Act, a company planning a capital expenditure of over ₦500,000 must notify the Industrial Inspectorate Division of the Federal Ministry of Trade and Investment. [24-26]

quizzes.scenario: SuperMart Ltd and GroceryHub Ltd plan to merge. Which body is the primary regulator responsible for ensuring the merger does not create a monopoly?
options: SEC
CAC
FCCPC
CBN
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act 2018, the FCCPC is the primary regulator for reviewing and approving mergers to prevent anti-competitive practices. [6, 17, 27]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign national, Hans, is hired as an IT expert by a Nigerian firm. Which agency grants the 'Expatriate Quota' required for him to work in Nigeria?
options: NIPC
NOTAP
Nigerian Immigration Service
Ministry of Foreign Affairs
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) manages the control of foreign nationals and grants expatriate quotas and work permits under the Immigration Act. [18, 23, 28]

quizzes.scenario: ABC Bank has a large portfolio of non-performing loans. Which corporation was established to purchase these toxic assets to stabilize the banking sector?
options: NDIC
CBN
AMCON
FIRS
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Asset Management Corporation of Nigeria (AMCON) was established to manage liquidity in banks by disposing of non-performing loans and toxic assets. [23, 29, 30]

quizzes.scenario: A company needs to stamp its Memorandum and Articles of Association during incorporation. Which agency is responsible for this?
options: CAC
Federal High Court
Federal Inland Revenue Service
Ministry of Justice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) is responsible for the collection of stamp duties and the stamping of incorporation documents. [18, 23]

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer called to the bar 2 years ago wants to incorporate a company for his client. He is not a director or shareholder of the proposed company. Can he file the documents?
options: Yes, as a legal practitioner
No, he must be accredited with the CAC first
Yes, if he has a seal
No, only the client can file
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Accreditation with the CAC is required for professionals (Legal Practitioners, Accountants, Secretaries) to provide incorporation services unless they are first shareholders or directors. [31-33]

quizzes.scenario: An accountant wants to act as a consultant for a public company's share issuance. He has 3 years of experience. Does he meet the SEC registration requirements?
options: Yes, he is an accountant
No, he needs 5 years post-call/qualification experience
No, he needs 10 years experience
Yes, if he passes the SEC test
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: SEC registration for individuals requires at least five years post-call/qualification experience and 4 years of corporate practice experience. [34, 35]

quizzes.scenario: A company's name is approved by the CAC, but it is later discovered the approval was obtained via fraudulent documents. What can the CAC do?
options: Nothing, the certificate is conclusive
Sue the company in court
Withdraw or cancel the approval of the name
Fine the directors only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 31(5) of CAMA 2020 empowers the Commission to withdraw or cancel a name reservation if discovered it was fraudulently or improperly procured. [36-38]

quizzes.scenario: An investor in the capital market has a dispute with his stockbroker regarding the unauthorized sale of shares. Which specialized body has jurisdiction?
options: Federal High Court
Investment and Securities Tribunal
Administrative Proceedings Committee of CAC
State High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) handles capital market disputes as established under the Investment and Securities Act. [39-41]

quizzes.scenario: A telecom company, Telco Nig Ltd, wishes to renew its operating license. Which specific sector regulator handles this?
options: NCC
NBC
SON
FCCPC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) is the regulatory body for the telecommunications sector under the NCC Act. [42, 43]

quizzes.scenario: An electricity distribution company, PowerGrid Ltd, is accused of unfair pricing. Which body regulates pricing and consumer protection in the power sector?
options: NERC
NCC
FCCPC
SON
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Electricity Regulatory Commission (NERC) regulates the electricity sector, including pricing and service delivery under the Electricity Act. [21, 43]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor wants to ensure his investment is protected against nationalization. Which Act provides this guarantee?
options: CAMA 2020
ISA 2025
NIPC Act
Foreign Exchange Act
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 25 of the NIPC Act protects foreign investments from asset nationalization or expropriation, ensuring guaranteed compensation. [44, 45]

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian entrepreneur wants to export cocoa to Europe. Which council provides incentives and promotes export trade?
options: NIPC
NEPC
SON
NAFDAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) is responsible for promoting and developing Nigeria's export trade and providing incentives. [46, 47]

quizzes.scenario: A company produces electric cables and needs to ensure they meet the minimum national industrial standards. Which body issues the mandatory certification?
options: NAFDAC
SON
FCCPC
NOTAP
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Standard Organization of Nigeria (SON) ensures that products manufactured in Nigeria meet specific quality standards under the SON Act. [6, 43]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company, Alpha Inc., is invited by the Federal Government to execute a specific power project in Delta State. Must they incorporate a local subsidiary?
options: Yes, Section 78 CAMA applies
No, they can apply for exemption under Section 80 CAMA
Yes, NIPC requires it
No, foreign companies are never registered
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 80(1)(a) of CAMA 2020 allows foreign companies invited by the Federal Government for specified projects to apply for exemption from local incorporation. [48, 49]

quizzes.scenario: The CAC refuses to register 'CleanEnergy Ltd' because it conflicts with a trademark. The applicant disagrees. What is the applicant's first step?
options: Sue the CAC in the Supreme Court
Give notice to the Commission to apply to the court for directions
File an action in the State High Court
Ignore the CAC and start business
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 41(2) of CAMA 2020, if aggrieved by a refusal to register, the applicant gives notice to the Commission, which must then apply to the court for directions within 21 days. [50, 51]

quizzes.scenario: A company is registered with a name that is 'suggestive of government patronage' without consent. Which internal CAC committee can adjudicate this dispute?
options: Audit Committee
Administrative Proceedings Committee
Remuneration Committee
Disciplinary Committee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Administrative Proceedings Committee (APC) of the CAC resolves non-criminal disputes, including those relating to prohibited or restricted names. [52-54]

quizzes.scenario: An exempted foreign company fails to deliver its 'Annual Report' to the CAC for the 2024 calendar year. Which form should they have used?
options: CAC Form 19
CAC Form 5
CAC Form 22
CAC Form 7
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Exempted foreign companies are required to deliver an Annual Report using the prescribed form (CAC Form 22) rather than the standard Annual Returns form. [55, 56]

quizzes.scenario: A company's registrar has failed to remit dividends to a shareholder. The shareholder wants to file a complaint. Which body handles such investment disputes?
options: Federal High Court
SEC Administrative Proceedings Committee
CAC Administrative Proceedings Committee
National Industrial Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC Administrative Proceedings Committee (APC) is a quasi-judicial body that hears perceived contraventions of the ISA or SEC Rules by capital market operators. [40, 57]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign investor brings 1 million USD into Nigeria through an authorized dealer. What document must they obtain to facilitate future repatriation of funds?
options: Certificate of Incorporation
Certificate of Capital Importation
Expatriate Quota
NIPC Registration
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to the Foreign Exchange (Monitoring and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, investors must import capital through authorized dealers and obtain a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI). [58, 59]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to verify if its competitor has registered a charge over its assets. What process should they initiate at the CAC?
options: Corporate Restructuring
Corporate Search
Winding Up
Annual Returns
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A corporate search (Manual or Electronic) at the CAC allows parties to confirm a company's details, including registered charges or encumbrances. [60, 61]

quizzes.scenario: A company in the 'upstream' petroleum sector is being investigated for environmental non-compliance. Which PIA body regulates upstream operations?
options: NMDPRA
NUPRC
NNPC
DPR
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) regulates upstream petroleum operations like exploration and drilling under the PIA. [29]

quizzes.scenario: A director of the CAC Governing Board is representing the accounting profession. Who appoints this representative?
options: The President
The Minister of Trade
ICAN
The Registrar General
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Members of the CAC Governing Board, including the representative of the accounting profession, are appointed by the Minister. [62, 63]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute involving a collective investment scheme (CIS) arises between the fund manager and unit holders. Which tribunal has original jurisdiction?
options: IST
FHC
NIC
CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Investment and Securities Tribunal (IST) has jurisdiction over disputes arising from the administration and management of collective investment schemes. [57, 64]

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner is handling a take-over bid for a public company. Is he a 'Capital Market Operator' or 'Capital Market Consultant'?
options: Operator
Consultant
Both
Neither
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Professionals like legal practitioners and accountants serving the capital market are generally classified as Capital Market Consultants under SEC rules. [65, 66]

quizzes.scenario: A company secretary of a public company must be qualified. Which of the following is NOT a valid qualification?
options: Legal Practitioner
Member of ICSAN
Member of ICAN
A director with 10 years business experience
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 332 of CAMA 2020 lists Legal Practitioners, Chartered Secretaries, and Chartered Accountants as qualified for public company secretaryship. [67-69]

quizzes.scenario: How many members constitute a quorum for a meeting of the CAC Governing Board?
options: Three
Five
Seven
Ten
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 5(3) of CAMA 2020 (as noted in S.A Badmus) specifies that the quorum for a meeting of the Commission's board is five. [54]

quizzes.scenario: A foreign company wishes to buy shares in a Nigerian company using convertible currency. Is this FDI or FPI?
options: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI)
Joint Venture
Acquisition
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Buying shares in an existing company without necessarily establishing a local business presence is classified as Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI). [59, 70]

quizzes.scenario: A dispute arises between a foreign investor and the Federal Government regarding an enterprise. What is the first mandated step for resolution?
options: Arbitration
Litigation in Federal High Court
Amicable settlement through mutual discussion
Petition to the President
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26 of the NIPC Act, parties to an investment dispute must first attempt to reach an amicable settlement through mutual discussion. [44, 71]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to use the word 'Holding' in its name. Whose consent is required before registration?
options: Attorney General
Registrar General of CAC
Minister of Trade
SEC Chairman
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Use of restricted words like 'Holding' or 'Group' requires the prior consent of the Registrar-General of the CAC. [43, 72, 73]

quizzes.scenario: An applicant for SEC registration is refused. How long does the SEC have to notify the applicant of the reasons for refusal?
options: 7 days
14 days
60 days
30 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The SEC has 60 days to review an application. If refused, it must notify the applicant and allow representation within 14 days. [74]

quizzes.scenario: A small company is being incorporated. Can they have only one director?
options: No, minimum is always two
Yes, CAMA 2020 allows it
Only if it is a foreign company
Only if the director is a lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that every company shall have at least two directors, except for a small company which may have one. [5, 75]

quizzes.scenario: Which regulatory body is responsible for managing the 'Combined Expatriate Residence Permit and Aliens Card' (CERPAC)?
options: NIPC
NIS
CAC
Ministry of Interior
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) issues the CERPAC to enable foreigners to live and work in Nigeria. [23, 76]

quizzes.scenario: A company fails to file annual returns for 10 consecutive years. What action can the CAC take?
options: Fine the shareholders
Arrest the Secretary
Strike the name of the company off the register
Seize the company's assets
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 425 of CAMA 2020, failure to file annual returns for 10 years is a ground for the Commission to strike the company off the register as defunct. [77, 78]

quizzes.scenario: A public company wants to appoint a director who is 75 years old. What is the requirement?
options: He cannot be appointed
He needs a court order
Special notice must be given to shareholders disclosing his age
Ordinary resolution without notice
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 requires special notice for the appointment of a director 70 years and above in a public company. [69, 79, 80]

quizzes.scenario: An aggrieved person wishes to appeal a decision of the Investment and Securities Tribunal. Where does the appeal go?
options: Federal High Court
Supreme Court
Court of Appeal
SEC APC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Appeals from the decisions of the IST lie to the Court of Appeal on points of law or mixed law and fact. [81, 82]

quizzes.scenario: A company intends to spend ₦100 million on R&D. Which tax incentive allows them to get a 20% credit on this expenditure?
options: Pioneer Status
Investment Tax Credit
Double Taxation Relief
Capital Allowance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Investment Tax Credit provides a 20% tax credit on funds spent by a company on research and development. [44]

quizzes.scenario: A newly called lawyer wants to register a business name for a client. Does he need to be accredited by the CAC?
options: Yes, all filings require accreditation
No, accreditation is only required for company incorporation under Part B
No, if he has a NBA seal
Yes, if the client is foreign
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Accreditation is specifically required for incorporating companies under Part B; anyone can register entities under Part E (Business Names) or F (Trustees). [83]

quizzes.scenario: Which body is responsible for granting 'Pioneer Status' which provides a tax holiday to certain industries?
options: FIRS
NIPC
CAC
Ministry of Finance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC) handles applications for Pioneer Status under the Industrial Development (Income Tax Relief) Act. [84, 85]

quizzes.scenario: A company's registered office is moved from Lagos to Abuja. Which form must be filed with the CAC to notify them of this change?
options: CAC Form 1.1
CAC Form 5
CAC Form 6
CAC Form 7
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Notice of a change in a company's registered address is filed using CAC Form 6. [86, 87]

quizzes.scenario: A company wants to appoint a new auditor to fill a casual vacancy. Who has the primary power to do this in the interim?
options: The Shareholders
The Managing Director
The Board of Directors
The CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 401(6) of CAMA 2020 empowers the Board of Directors to fill a casual vacancy in the office of an auditor. [88]

quizzes.scenario: BlueWave Inc., a French company, has been invited by the Federal Government of Nigeria to execute a specific specialist bridge construction project in Delta State. The company does not wish to incorporate a local subsidiary. What is the most appropriate legal step?
options: They must incorporate as a separate entity under S.78 CAMA
They should apply to the Minister of Trade for an exemption under S.80 CAMA
They can operate using their French certificate of incorporation
They must register a Business Name with the CAC
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 80(1)(a) of the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA) 2020, a foreign company invited by the Federal Government to execute a specified individual project may apply to the Minister of Trade, Industry and Investment for an exemption from the mandatory requirement to incorporate a local entity [1-3].

quizzes.scenario: Hans, a German national, intends to set up a business in Nigeria solely for the production of military camouflage and tactical gear for private security firms. He approaches you for legal advice. What would you tell him?
options: He can proceed if he registers with the NIPC
He is prohibited as this falls under the Negative List
He only needs a Business Permit from the NIS
He must partner with a Nigerian citizen
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 18 and 31 of the NIPC Act and Section 126 of CAMA 2020, foreigners are prohibited from investing in enterprises listed on the 'Negative List,' which includes the production of military and paramilitary wears and accoutrement [4-6].

quizzes.scenario: A Chinese investment group successfully incorporates 'Dragon Steel Ltd' with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC). They intend to begin operations immediately. What is the mandatory next step before they can legally commence business?
options: Registration with the Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC)
Obtaining a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI)
Applying for a Business Permit from the NIS
Registering the technology transfer agreement with NOTAP
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 20 of the NIPC Act, every enterprise in which foreign participation is involved must, after its incorporation, be registered with the NIPC before it can legally commence business in Nigeria [7-9].

quizzes.scenario: TechFlow Ltd, a Nigerian company, enters into a licensing agreement with a US software firm to use proprietary coding for 10 years. The agreement was executed on January 1st. By what date must this contract be registered with NOTAP?
options: Within 30 days of execution
Within 60 days of execution
Within 90 days of execution
Before any payment is made to the US firm
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the National Office for Technology Acquisition and Promotion (NOTAP) Act, any contract or agreement involving the transfer of foreign technology must be registered with NOTAP within 60 days of its execution or conclusion [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: An American investor, Sarah, brings $5 million into Nigeria through Zenith Bank to fund her new startup. She wants to ensure she can eventually repatriate her profits in US Dollars. What document must she obtain?
options: A Business Permit
A NIPC Registration Certificate
A Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI)
An Expatriate Quota
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 15 of the Foreign Exchange (Monitoring and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act, an authorized dealer (bank) through which capital is imported must issue a Certificate of Capital Importation (CCI), which guarantees the unconditional transferability and repatriation of profits and capital in a freely convertible currency [13-15].

quizzes.scenario: GlobalOil PLC, a UK-based company, intends to post six of its top British engineers to manage its new subsidiary in Port Harcourt. Who is responsible for applying for the expatriate quota?
options: The six British engineers individually
The British Embassy in Nigeria
The Nigerian subsidiary company (employer)
The Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 2 of the Immigration Act and judicial precedent in Oil Field Supply Centre v Johnson, the duty to apply for an expatriate quota lies with the company wishing to employ the foreigners, not the individual employees themselves [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: A Canadian firm, MapleSoft, begins selling software directly in Lagos through a local office without incorporating a local entity or obtaining an exemption. They sign a contract with a local distributor. What is the legal status of this contract?
options: Valid and enforceable
Voidable at the option of MapleSoft
Void and illegal
Enforceable only if registered with NIPC later
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 78(2) of CAMA 2020, any act or transaction of a foreign company carried out in Nigeria before it is incorporated as a local entity (unless exempted) is void and a nullity [18-20].

quizzes.scenario: An Italian investor has a dispute with the Federal Government regarding his manufacturing plant. They have tried to resolve it via mutual discussion but failed. They did not specify a method in their agreement. What rules apply to resolve the conflict?
options: The Arbitration and Mediation Act 2023
The rules of the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID)
The High Court Civil Procedure Rules
The NIPC Administrative Rules
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 26 of the NIPC Act, where there is a disagreement between an investor and the Federal Government as to the method of dispute settlement to be adopted, the rules of the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID) shall apply [21-23].

quizzes.scenario: Bjorn, a Swedish national, does not want to manage a company but wants to buy 10% of the shares in a public company listed on the Nigerian Exchange. What form of investment is this?
options: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI)
Joint Venture Investment
Exempted Foreign Investment
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Foreign Portfolio Investment (FPI) involves the purchase of financial assets such as shares or stocks in Nigerian companies without the investor seeking active management or control [24, 25].

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian company fails to register its technical service agreement with NOTAP within the statutory period. The foreign partner is demanding payment. What is the legal effect of this non-registration?
options: The entire contract is void
The foreign partner cannot sue in Nigeria
It only prevents the transfer of fees through the official foreign exchange market
The Nigerian company's directors are criminally liable
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 7 of the NOTAP Act and Stanbic IBTC v FRCN, non-registration of a technology transfer agreement does not make it void but prevents the repatriation of funds/fees due under the contract to a foreign account through the Central Bank of Nigeria [26-28].

subtopics.subtopicName: Choice of Business Organisation and Formation of Companies

quizzes.scenario: Segun, aged 16, and his father, Chief Ade, decide to incorporate a private company, 'Ade & Sons Ltd.' They are the only subscribers to the memorandum. The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) refuses the registration. Why is the CAC right?
options: An infant is completely prohibited from forming a company under CAMA
A private company must have at least two adult subscribers if an infant is involved
A minor cannot be a member of any company in Nigeria
Segun must be at least 18 years old to be a subscriber in any circumstance
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20(2) of CAMA 2020, an infant may join in forming a company provided there are at least two other subscribers of full age and capacity. In this scenario, there is only one adult subscriber (Chief Ade). [1-3]

quizzes.scenario: Alhaji Buba was declared an undischarged bankrupt by a High Court in 2023. He intends to join his friends in forming a new tech company, 'Z-Tech Ltd,' as a subscriber. What is his legal status regarding this formation?
options: He is eligible since he is over 18 years old
He is disqualified unless he obtains leave from the court
He is disqualified until he is discharged from bankruptcy
He can only join if the other subscribers are not bankrupt
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 20(1)(c) of CAMA 2020 specifically disqualifies an undischarged bankrupt from taking part in the formation of a company. [2, 4, 5]

quizzes.scenario: Three investors want to incorporate 'Swift Logistics Ltd' as a private company. They propose an issued share capital of ₦60,000. Will the CAC register this company?
options: Yes, provided they pay the registration fees
No, the minimum issued share capital for a private company is ₦100,000
No, the minimum issued share capital for a private company is ₦500,000
Yes, if it is a small company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020 provides that the minimum issued share capital for a private company shall not be less than ₦100,000. [6-8]

quizzes.scenario: 'Oceanic Oil Plc' is being formed as a public company. The promoters propose an initial issued share capital of ₦1,500,000. Is this proposal compliant with CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, since it exceeds the private company threshold
No, public companies must have a minimum issued share capital of ₦2,000,000
No, public companies must have a minimum issued share capital of ₦5,000,000
Yes, provided it is fully paid up
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020, the minimum issued share capital for a public company is ₦2,000,000. [7, 9, 10]

quizzes.scenario: 'Alpha Ventures Ltd,' a private company, currently has 48 members. It intends to issue shares to 5 new employees who will become shareholders. Will this breach the membership limit for private companies?
options: Yes, because the membership will exceed 50
No, because employees and former employees who are members are excluded from the limit
No, because private companies can have up to 100 members under the 2020 Act
Yes, unless they become joint holders
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) of CAMA 2020 limits the membership of a private company to 50, but provides that employees and former employees who became members during their employment are not counted toward this limit. [6, 11, 12]

quizzes.scenario: Three siblings, Funmi, Tunde, and Bolaji, hold 1,000 units of shares jointly in 'Siblings Trading Ltd,' a private company. For the purpose of determining the membership limit, how many members are they counted as?
options: Three members
Two members
One member
They are not counted until the shares are split
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(4) of CAMA 2020 states that where two or more persons hold one or more shares in a company jointly, they shall be treated as a single member for the purpose of the membership limit. [6, 11]

quizzes.scenario: A group of alumni wants to form 'Great Scholars Ltd/Gte' to promote education. They intend to include a clause allowing the distribution of 10% of annual profits as dividends to members. Is this permissible?
options: Yes, as long as the majority of profit goes to the object
No, profits of a company limited by guarantee must be applied solely toward its objects
Yes, if the Attorney General of the Federation consents
No, companies limited by guarantee cannot make profit
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 26(1) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provide that the income and property of a company limited by guarantee shall be applied solely toward the promotion of its objects, and no portion shall be paid or transferred directly or indirectly to members as dividends. [13-15]

quizzes.scenario: Promoters of a charitable foundation applied to the Attorney General of the Federation (AGF) for consent to register 'Health for All Ltd/Gte.' 45 days have passed without a decision from the AGF. What is the next legal step?
options: They must wait indefinitely for the AGF's consent
They should assume consent is denied and sue the AGF
They must advertise the application in three national daily newspapers to invite objections
They can proceed to register with the CAC directly without further steps
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 26(7) and (8) of CAMA 2020, if the AGF does not decide within 30 days, promoters shall advertise the application in three national newspapers and, if no objections arise within 28 days, the CAC may proceed with registration. [16, 17]

quizzes.scenario: 'Unity Bank' wants to incorporate a public limited company. They propose the name 'Unity Global Ltd.' Is this name appropriate?
options: Yes, 'Ltd' is a standard abbreviation for all companies
No, the name of a public company limited by shares must end with 'Public Limited Company' or 'Plc'
Yes, if it is a subsidiary of a private company
No, 'Ltd' can only be used by companies limited by guarantee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 29(2) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that the name of a public company limited by shares shall end with the words 'Public Limited Company' or the abbreviation 'Plc'. [18-20]

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Obi is the sole shareholder of 'Obi Graphics Ltd.' The company owes a debt of ₦5 million to a supplier. The supplier sues Mr. Obi personally for the debt. What is the likely outcome?
options: Mr. Obi is liable because he is the sole owner
The suit will fail because the company is a separate legal entity from Mr. Obi
Mr. Obi is liable up to the amount of his personal assets
The supplier must sue both Mr. Obi and the company jointly
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Based on the principle of corporate personality established in Salomon v Salomon and recognized under Section 42 of CAMA 2020, an incorporated company is a juristic entity distinct from its members. [21-24]

quizzes.scenario: Omega Ltd recently incorporated and has its registered office in Lagos. The directors decide to display the company's name only on the internal reception desk and not at the entrance of the building.
options: This is lawful as long as the name is inside the building
The company is only required to publish its name on official documents
The company must paint or affix its name in a conspicuous position on the outside of every office or place of business
The company only needs to display the name at its headquarters
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 729(1)(a) of CAMA 2020, every company must paint or affix its name on the outside of every office or place of business in a conspicuous position and in letters easily legible [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of Swift Logistics Ltd wants to change the company's Articles of Association to remove the requirement for a common seal. They pass a Board Resolution to this effect.
options: The change is valid as it is a board matter
The change is invalid because the Articles can only be altered by a Special Resolution of the members
The change is valid but must be confirmed by the CAC
The change is invalid because every company must have a common seal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 53(1) of CAMA 2020, a company may only alter its Articles of Association by passing a Special Resolution in a general meeting [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: A shareholder of Alpha Ltd, a private company, wishes to inspect the register of members. The company secretary refuses, stating that the register is currently being kept at the Chairman's private residence for safekeeping.
options: The secretary is right to protect the register
The register must be kept at the company's registered office and be open for inspection
The register can be kept anywhere as long as the CAC is notified
Only directors are allowed to inspect the register
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 110(1) of CAMA 2020 requires that the register of members be kept at the company's registered office, and Section 113 provides that it shall be open to the inspection of any member [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Beta Plc wishes to change its name to 'Beta Oil & Gas Plc'. The members pass a Special Resolution, but the company secretary argues they also need the written consent of the Corporate Affairs Commission.
options: The secretary is wrong; a Special Resolution is sufficient
The secretary is right; the change of name requires the written consent of the Commission
The company needs a court order, not CAC consent
Only a board resolution is needed for a name change
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 30(1) of CAMA 2020, a company may change its name by special resolution and with the written approval of the Commission [7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Gamma Ltd passes a Special Resolution to alter its objects clause to include real estate development. Mr. Ade, who holds 18% of the company's shares, voted against the resolution and wants to challenge it in court.
options: Mr. Ade cannot challenge a majority decision
Mr. Ade can apply to the Federal High Court for the cancellation of the alteration within 28 days
Mr. Ade must apply to the CAC first for a review
Mr. Ade can only challenge the alteration if he holds 51% of the shares
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 51(1) and (2) of CAMA 2020 allows holders of not less than 15% of the nominal value of the company's issued share capital to apply to the court for the cancellation of an alteration to the objects within 28 days of passing the resolution [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: Delta Ltd, a private company, has 45 members. It wants to re-register as a public company (Plc). The directors realize their current issued share capital is ₦500,000.
options: They can re-register as a Plc with the current capital
They must first increase their issued share capital to at least ₦2,000,000
Public companies do not have a minimum share capital requirement
They only need to change the name to 'Delta Plc'
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 27(2)(a) of CAMA 2020 provides that the minimum issued share capital for a public company shall not be less than ₦2,000,000 [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Epsilon Plc wants to convert into a private company (Ltd). A group of members holding 6% of the company's shares opposes the move.
options: They cannot stop the conversion as the majority rules
They can apply to the court for the cancellation of the resolution within 28 days
Only the CAC can cancel the conversion
They must hold at least 15% to object
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 64(1) of CAMA 2020, members holding at least 5% in nominal value of the company's issued share capital may apply to the court for the cancellation of a resolution to re-register a public company as private [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Zeta Ltd, a company limited by shares, intends to re-register as an unlimited company. The Board holds a meeting and passes a Special Resolution with 75% support.
options: The conversion is validly approved
The conversion requires the assent of all members of the company
The conversion is prohibited under CAMA 2020
Unlimited companies do not require member consent
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 68(1)(b) of CAMA 2020 specifies that a company limited by shares may re-register as unlimited provided all the members assent to its being so re-registered [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: An investor wants to verify if 'Titanium Ltd' has any registered charges on its assets before granting a loan. He instructs his lawyer to conduct a search.
options: The lawyer must apply to the Federal High Court
The lawyer should conduct a corporate search at the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC)
Charges are not public record
The search must be conducted at the Land Registry only
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Corporate searches are conducted at the Corporate Affairs Commission to verify a company's status, including registered charges and encumbrances [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Theta Ltd, a small company, was incorporated on June 1, 2023. The directors are unsure when they are required to file their first annual return.
options: They must file by June 30, 2023
They are not required to file an annual return in the year of incorporation or the following year
They must file within 42 days of incorporation
Annual returns are only for public companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 417 of CAMA 2020 and relevant regulations, small companies are exempted from filing annual returns in the year of incorporation or the year following incorporation [19, 20].

quizzes.scenario: Ade, a 17-year-old tech prodigy, wants to join the formation of Zenith PLC alongside two adults who are not disqualified. Can Ade validly subscribe to the memorandum?
options: Yes, because there are two other persons not disqualified subscribing to the memorandum
No, because he is an infant and cannot be a member
Only if he has a court order
Only if the company is a private company
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 20(2) of CAMA 2020, a person shall not be disqualified as an infant if two other persons not disqualified have subscribed to the memorandum [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Blue-Ray Ltd, a private company, has 48 individual members. They want to admit a husband and wife who will hold their shares jointly. Will this exceed the membership limit?
options: Yes, they will have 50 members
No, joint holders are treated as a single member
Yes, unless they are employees
No, because the limit is 100 for private companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 22(3) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provides that the membership limit for a private company is 50, and two or more persons holding shares jointly are treated as a single member [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: Chidi subscribed to the memorandum of Alpha Ltd during incorporation, but the company secretary forgot to enter his name in the register of members. Is Chidi a member?
options: No, until his name is entered in the register
Yes, subscribers are deemed members upon registration of the company
Only if he has paid for the shares
Only if he attends the first AGM
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 105(1) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that subscribers to the memorandum are deemed to have agreed to become members and shall be entered as members upon registration, regardless of the register entry [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: A creditor of Delta Ltd converts his ₦10 million loan into equity shares by agreement. At what point does he legally become a member of the company?
options: Upon signing the debt-equity agreement
Upon issuance of the share certificate
When his name is entered in the register of members
After the next AGM
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 105(2) of CAMA 2020, persons who agree in writing to become members become so when their names are entered in the register of members [5, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bala, a member of Gasket Ltd, has been declared of unsound mind by a High Court in Lagos. What is the immediate effect on his membership?
options: He automatically ceases to be a member
His shares are forfeited to the company
He cannot exercise membership rights personally, and his shares vest in a committee or trustee
The company must wind up
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 106(1) and (4) of CAMA 2020 provides that a person of unsound mind found so by a court cannot become a member, and if already a member, his shares vest in a representative [5, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Efe holds 6% of the voting rights in a public company. Is the company required to include his name in the register of persons with significant control?
options: No, the threshold is 10%
Yes, the threshold is at least 5%
Only if he is also a director
Only if he is a foreigner
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 119 of CAMA 2020 requires every company to keep a register of persons with significant control, defined as holding at least 5% of shares or voting rights [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: Fola, a member of a public company, wants to inspect the minutes book of the last AGM. The company secretary refuses, stating it is confidential. Is the secretary right?
options: Yes, minutes are for directors only
No, members have a statutory right to inspect the minute book for at least 6 hours daily without charge
Yes, if Fola owns less than 10% shares
No, but Fola must pay a ₦5,000 inspection fee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 266 and 267 of CAMA 2020 grant members the right to inspect the minute book of general meetings at the registered office for at least 6 hours a day without charge [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Global PLC was incorporated on February 1, 2024. By what date must they hold their statutory meeting?
options: August 1, 2024
February 1, 2025
December 31, 2024
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 235(1) of CAMA 2020 requires every public company to hold a statutory meeting within six months of its incorporation [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Horizon Ltd fail to deliver the statutory report to the CAC or hold the statutory meeting as required. What can the members do?
options: Nothing, it is a board matter
Petition the court for the winding up of the company
Sue the CAC for negligence
Automatically remove the directors
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 571(b) of CAMA 2020 provides that default in delivering the statutory report or holding the statutory meeting is a ground for compulsory winding up by the court [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: J-Coy Ltd was incorporated in June 2023. They held their first AGM in December 2024 (18 months later). When is the latest they can hold the next AGM?
options: December 2025
March 2026
September 2025
June 2025
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 237 of CAMA 2020 requires an AGM every year, and not more than 15 months shall elapse between one AGM and the next [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Alpha Ltd, a large logistics company with a turnover of 500 million Naira, currently has only one director. The board wants to know if this is legal under CAMA 2020.
options: It is legal as long as they notify the CAC.
It is illegal as only small companies can have one director.
It is legal if the director is also the secretary.
It is illegal because all companies must have at least three directors.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 271(1) of CAMA 2020, every company except a small company must have at least two directors. Alpha Ltd does not qualify as a small company due to its turnover exceeding 120 million Naira [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: During the incorporation of Zenith Tech Ltd, the subscribers failed to name the first directors in the Memorandum. Where should these names be sought?
options: In the Articles of Association.
In a separate board resolution.
From the Corporate Affairs Commission.
In the shareholders' agreement.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 272 of CAMA 2020, if the first directors are not named in the Memorandum, they may be named in the Articles of Association [2, 3].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. John, an undischarged bankrupt, wants to be appointed as a director in a new fintech startup. Can he be validly appointed?
options: Yes, provided he discloses his bankruptcy.
Yes, if the other directors approve.
No, he is disqualified by law.
No, unless he owns at least 50% of the shares.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 283(c) of CAMA 2020, an undischarged bankrupt is expressly disqualified from being appointed as a director of a company [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: A public company, Globe PLC, intends to appoint Chief Obafemi, who is 75 years old, to its board. What procedural requirement must be met?
options: A special resolution must be passed.
A special notice must be given to members stating his age.
Unanimous consent of the board is required.
No special procedure is required for directors over 70.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 282 of CAMA 2020 requires that for a public company, special notice (28 days) must be given of any resolution appointing a director who is 70 years of age or older, and the notice must disclose his age [6, 7].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Emeka is currently a director in six different public companies. According to CAMA 2020, what is the consequence of this arrangement?
options: He must resign from all but five at the next AGM.
He can remain in all if he pays a fine to the CAC.
He is automatically disqualified from all six.
There is no limit on the number of public directorships.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 307(2) of CAMA 2020 provides that a person shall not be a director in more than five public companies, and any person currently holding more must resign from the excess not later than the next AGM [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: The board of Omega Ltd consists of 9 directors. At a board meeting, only 2 directors are present. Can they validly transact business?
options: Yes, 2 is the default quorum for all companies.
No, the quorum must be one-third of the total number of directors.
Yes, if the Chairman is one of the two.
No, the quorum is fixed at 5 for a board of 9.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 290 of CAMA 2020, if the number of directors is more than 6, the quorum is one-third of the total number, which for 9 directors would be 3 [10-12].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Ade is not officially appointed as a director of Beta Ltd, but the board of Beta Ltd is accustomed to following his specific instructions. What is Mr. Ade's legal status?
options: He is a Managing Director.
He is a Shadow Director.
He is an Interloper Director.
He has no legal status as he wasn't appointed.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 270 of CAMA 2020 defines a shadow director as any person on whose instructions and directions the directors are accustomed to act [13-15].

quizzes.scenario: The members of Delta Ltd are unhappy with the performance of Director Kalu and wish to remove him before his term expires. What is the correct procedure?
options: A board resolution is sufficient.
An ordinary resolution with special notice.
A special resolution is required.
Only the court can remove a director before his term ends.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 288(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that a company may by ordinary resolution remove a director before the expiration of his period of office, notwithstanding anything in its articles, provided special notice (28 days) is given [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Musa is a Life Director at Zeta Ltd. The members want to remove him due to gross misconduct. Chief Musa claims he cannot be removed because of his 'Life' status. Is he correct?
options: Yes, Life Directors are permanent.
No, he can be removed via Section 288.
Yes, unless he is convicted of a crime.
No, but only if the Articles are first amended.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 281 of CAMA 2020, a person may be appointed a life director, but he is still subject to removal under Section 288 like any other director [13, 17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Bello, a director of Shine Ltd, resides in London. The company is holding a board meeting in Lagos. Is the company required to send him a notice of the meeting?
options: Yes, all directors must receive notice.
No, unless he has provided an address in Nigeria for service.
Yes, but only if the meeting is an AGM.
No, directors outside Nigeria are never entitled to notice.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 292(2) of CAMA 2020, it is not necessary to give notice of a meeting to a director who is absent from Nigeria unless he has provided an address in Nigeria for such purposes [11, 19].

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of Zandora Ltd, a private company, has just concluded its first meeting after incorporation. They are deliberating on the requirement to set a date for the company's financial year-end.
options: The directors must set the date at their first meeting and notify the CAC within 14 days.
The directors have 6 months to decide on the date.
The date is automatically December 31st of every year.
Only public companies are required to set a financial year-end.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 377(4) of CAMA 2020 provides that directors shall at their first meeting determine the date to which the financial statements shall be made up and shall, within 14 days of the determination, notify the Commission of that date. [1], [2]

quizzes.scenario: GreenLife Ltd has been operating for 7 years. The company secretary suggests disposing of all accounting records from the first three years of operations to create space in the archive.
options: Records must be kept for at least 10 years.
Records must be kept for at least 6 years from the date they were made.
Records can be disposed of after the annual audit.
Only public companies are required to preserve records for more than 2 years.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Pursuant to Section 375(2) of CAMA 2020, accounting records shall be preserved by the company for a period of at least six years from the date on which they were made. [3]

quizzes.scenario: Chief Okafor, a director in Zenith PLC, wishes to inspect the company's accounting records at the registered office on a Tuesday morning. The company secretary refuses, stating only the auditor can see them.
options: The secretary is correct; only auditors have a right of access.
Any member of the public can inspect records.
All officers of the company, including directors, have a right to inspect records at all times.
Only shareholders with more than 10% stake can inspect records.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 375(1) of CAMA 2020, accounting records shall at all times be open to inspection by the officers of the company. Section 868 defines officers to include directors and secretaries. [3]

quizzes.scenario: BlueRay Ltd, a private company, is preparing its annual financial statements. The accountant insists on including a statement of cash flow and a five-year financial summary.
options: These are mandatory for all companies under CAMA.
Private companies are exempted from including a statement of cash flow and a five-year financial summary.
Only small companies are exempted, not all private companies.
These are only required if the company has foreign participation.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 377(3) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that the financial statements of a private company need not include the statement of cash flow, changes in equity, value-added statement, and five-year financial summary. [4], [5]

quizzes.scenario: The Board of Directors of MegaCorp PLC has prepared the company's balance sheet. To save time, only the Managing Director signs the document before it is sent to the CAC.
options: The MD's signature alone is sufficient for public companies.
The balance sheet must be signed by at least two directors on behalf of the board.
The balance sheet must be signed by all directors.
The signature of the company secretary is the only legal requirement.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 386(1) of CAMA 2020 requires that a company's balance sheet shall be signed on behalf of the board by two of the directors of the company. [6]

quizzes.scenario: Apex Holdings, a company with three subsidiaries, is preparing its year-end accounts. The directors decide not to prepare group financial statements because the subsidiaries operate in different industries.
options: This is a valid reason for non-consolidation.
Group financial statements are mandatory for all holding companies without exception.
Holding companies must prepare group financial statements unless it is impracticable or misleading.
Only the CAC can waive the requirement for group financial statements.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 379 of CAMA 2020 provides that if a company has subsidiaries at the end of its financial year, group financial statements shall be prepared, except where it is impracticable, of no value to members, or would be misleading. [7], [8]

quizzes.scenario: Smart Finance Ltd is a small company. The directors want to submit modified financial statements to the CAC to simplify the filing process.
options: Small companies cannot submit modified statements.
Only the profit and loss account can be modified.
Small companies may deliver modified financial statements including an abbreviated balance sheet.
Modified statements are only for companies limited by guarantee.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 393 of CAMA 2020 allows a small company to deliver modified financial statements, and Section 350-353 permits the modified balance sheet to be an abbreviated version. [9], [10]

quizzes.scenario: A shareholder of Alpha PLC demands to see the company's last financial statements three weeks before the AGM. The company demands a fee for the copy.
options: The company can charge a maximum of N100 per page.
Any member is entitled to be furnished with a copy on demand and without charge.
Only members who have been shareholders for 2 years have this right.
The right to copies only exists during the AGM.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 391 of CAMA 2020, any member or debenture holder is entitled to be furnished on demand and without charge with a copy of the company's last financial statements. [11]

quizzes.scenario: NewTech Ltd has just been incorporated. The promoters want to know who is responsible for appointing the first auditors before the company starts business.
options: The Corporate Affairs Commission appoints the first auditors.
The members at the first Annual General Meeting.
The directors may appoint the first auditors at any time before the company commences business.
The company secretary is responsible for the appointment.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 401(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that the first auditors of a company may be appointed by the directors at any time before the company is entitled to commence business. [12], [13]

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Prime PLC failed to appoint auditors before the company commenced business. The company has now reached its first AGM.
options: The directors lose their right to vote at the AGM.
The company must pay a fine to the CAC.
The members at the general meeting may appoint the auditors where the directors have failed to do so.
The CAC will automatically appoint an auditor for the company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 401(5) of CAMA 2020 states that if the directors fail to exercise their powers to appoint first auditors, the company in general meeting may appoint them. [12], [13]

subtopics.subtopicName: Shares, Debentures and Enforcement of Securities

quizzes.scenario: BlueChip Ltd, a private company, allots 10,000 ordinary shares to Mr. Emeka on June 1. By what date must the company file the return of allotment with the CAC?
options: June 15
June 30
July 1
July 15
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: According to Section 154 of CAMA 2020, as amended by Section 6 of the Business Facilitation Act 2022, a company must file a return of allotment in Form CAC 5 within 15 days of the allotment [1-3].

quizzes.scenario: Zenith Plc intends to issue shares to an expert in exchange for intellectual property. What is the mandatory step regarding the valuation of this non-cash consideration?
options: The directors must value it themselves
An independent valuer must provide a valuation report
The CAC must determine the value
No valuation is required for public companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 162 of CAMA 2020 requires that where a public company allots shares for a consideration other than cash, the consideration must be valued by an independent valuer who shall submit a report to the company [4-6].

quizzes.scenario: A director at Alpha Ltd suggests issuing 1,000 ordinary shares at a nominal value of N1.00 for N0.80 to attract investors. Is this proposal legally sound?
options: Yes, if authorized by the articles
Yes, if approved by a special resolution
No, it is generally prohibited
Yes, if the court confirms it
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 146 of CAMA 2020 strictly prohibits the issuance of shares at a discount, meaning shares cannot be sold for less than their nominal value [7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: Star Ventures Ltd issues N1.00 shares at N1.50. Where should the extra N0.50 per share be recorded in the company's books?
options: General Reserve Account
Retained Earnings
Share Premium Account
Asset Revaluation Account
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 145(2) of CAMA 2020 stipulates that when shares are issued at a premium, a sum equal to the aggregate amount of the value of the premiums must be transferred to the 'Share Premium Account' [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: The board of directors of Mega Corp PLC wishes to use funds from the Share Premium Account. Which of the following is a lawful use of these funds?
options: Paying annual directors' fees
Funding a political campaign
Paying up unissued shares to be issued as fully paid bonus shares
Buying a new private jet for the CEO
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 145(4)(a) of CAMA 2020 permits the use of the Share Premium Account for paying up unissued shares of the company to be issued to members as fully paid bonus shares [7, 10].

quizzes.scenario: To maintain absolute control, the majority shareholder of XYZ Ltd proposes creating a class of shares that allows 5 votes per share. Is this permissible under CAMA 2020?
options: Yes, if it is a private company
No, weighted shares are generally prohibited
Yes, if the SEC approves
Yes, for all public companies
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 140(1) of CAMA 2020 prohibits the issuance of weighted or non-voting shares, providing that every share shall carry one vote except in specific circumstances like preference shares [9, 11].

quizzes.scenario: Mr. Musa, a shareholder in a private company, dies intestate. His son, the administrator of his estate, wishes to be registered as the holder of the shares. What process applies?
options: Allotment
Transfer
Transmission
Forfeiture
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 179 of CAMA 2020 provides for the transmission of shares, which is an automatic transfer of ownership by operation of law due to death or bankruptcy of a member [12, 13].

quizzes.scenario: A shareholder in Swift Logistics Ltd has not paid the final call on his shares despite several notices. What action can the directors take if authorized by the articles?
options: Sue for criminal conversion
Forfeit the shares
Sell the company's head office
Increase the nominal value of the shares
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 165 of CAMA 2020, if a member fails to pay any call or installment on the day appointed for payment, the directors may, if authorized by the articles, pass a resolution for the forfeiture of such shares [14, 15].

quizzes.scenario: Global Tech PLC issues a prospectus for a public offer. Mr. Ade relies on a false statement in the prospectus and suffers a financial loss. Who can he sue for compensation?
options: Only the company
The CAC
The company and every director who authorized the issue
Only the issuing house
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 113 of the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) 2025, the company, every director, and every person who authorized the issue are liable to compensate subscribers for losses resulting from misstatements in a prospectus [16, 17].

quizzes.scenario: A private company, SmallBiz Ltd, wishes to help its manager buy shares in the company by giving him a low-interest loan. Is this allowed?
options: No, financial assistance is always illegal
Yes, if the company's net assets are not reduced below 50% and it is approved by special resolution
Yes, only if it is a public company
No, only banks can do this
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 183(4) of CAMA 2020 allows private companies to provide financial assistance for the acquisition of their own shares if their net assets are not reduced or the assistance is from distributable profits, approved by special resolution [18, 19].

quizzes.scenario: The directors of Zenith Ltd are accused of misappropriating company funds for personal vacations. At a general meeting, a majority of the shareholders vote to ratify the directors' actions and forgo legal proceedings. Mr. Okoro, a minority shareholder, wishes to sue the directors on behalf of the company.
options: Mr. Okoro can sue as of right because he is a member.
Mr. Okoro cannot sue because the majority has ratified the conduct under the rule in Foss v Harbottle.
Mr. Okoro must seek the consent of the Corporate Affairs Commission.
Mr. Okoro can only sue if he holds 15% of the shares.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under the Proper Plaintiff Rule in Section 341 of CAMA 2020 (the rule in Foss v Harbottle), where a wrong is done to a company, the company is the proper plaintiff and the court will not interfere if the act is ratifiable by the majority [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: The board of directors of Peak Plc decides to invest company funds in a business object that is expressly prohibited by the company's Memorandum of Association. Sarah, a minority shareholder, objects and wishes to restrain the company.
options: Sarah cannot sue as she is a minority shareholder.
Sarah can bring a direct action for an injunction to restrain the ultra vires act.
Sarah must first get the majority to vote against the act at a general meeting.
Sarah must apply for the winding up of the company.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 343(a) of CAMA 2020 provides an exception to the majority rule, allowing a member to bring a direct action for an injunction or declaration to restrain the company from entering into an illegal or ultra vires transaction [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: Chief Bolaji, a member of Delta Ltd, is consistently denied his right to receive notices of general meetings and is blocked from voting. He wishes to sue the directors for this specific breach.
options: Chief Bolaji must bring a derivative action on behalf of the company.
Chief Bolaji must bring a personal action to enforce his individual rights.
Chief Bolaji cannot sue as the decision to exclude him is an internal management issue.
Chief Bolaji must wait for the next AGM to raise the issue.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 344(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a member to bring a personal action to enforce a right due to him personally, such as the right to notice and the right to vote [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Directors of Alpha Ltd, who are also majority shareholders, sell company property to their own private firm at 20% of its market value. A minority shareholder, Jude, wants to challenge this.
options: Jude cannot sue because the directors are the majority and can ratify the sale.
Jude can sue because this constitutes 'fraud on the minority' where the wrongdoers are in control.
Jude must apply to the CAC for investigation first.
Jude can only sue if he holds at least 25% of the share capital.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 343(d) of CAMA 2020 provides that a minority shareholder can sue where a fraud is being committed on the company and the wrongdoers are the controllers of the company [7].

quizzes.scenario: A group of minority shareholders in Beta Ltd believe the managing director has breached his fiduciary duties. They wish to sue him in the name of the company because the board refuses to take action.
options: They must apply to the Federal High Court for leave to bring a derivative action.
They can sue directly in their own names for damages.
They must pass a special resolution at a general meeting first.
They must report the matter to the Nigerian Police Force.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 346(1) of CAMA 2020 provides that a member may apply to the court for leave to bring an action in the name and on behalf of the company (derivative action) when the company itself fails to do so [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: Tunde wishes to bring a derivative action against the directors of Global Plc. Before filing, he must satisfy certain conditions regarding notice.
options: He must give the directors at least 14 days' notice of his intention.
He must give the directors reasonable notice of his intention to apply to the court for leave.
He must publish the notice in two national newspapers.
No notice is required for derivative actions.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 346(2) of CAMA 2020 requires that the applicant for a derivative action must give reasonable notice to the directors of his intention to apply to the court if they do not bring the action [10, 11].

quizzes.scenario: In a derivative action brought by a shareholder, the court finds the directors liable for negligence. Where should the awarded damages be paid?
options: Directly to the shareholder who brought the action.
Into the company's accounts.
To the Corporate Affairs Commission.
Distributed among the minority shareholders only.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: In a derivative action, the shareholder is enforcing a right that belongs to the company; therefore, the relief granted, such as damages, accrues to the company [9, 12].

quizzes.scenario: A shareholder of Omega Ltd brings a derivative action in good faith. The court finds the action was in the best interest of the company but dismisses the claim on a technicality. Who pays the legal fees?
options: The shareholder must pay all costs.
The court may order the company to pay the reasonable legal fees incurred by the applicant.
The directors must pay the fees personally.
The Federal Government pays the fees from the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 347(2)(d) of CAMA 2020 empowers the court to order the company to pay reasonable legal fees incurred by the applicant in a derivative proceeding [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: Musa, a minority shareholder, alleges that the majority shareholders are running the company in a way that ignores his interests and excludes him from all dividends while they pay themselves massive 'consultancy fees.' What is his best course of action?
options: Apply for an investigation of the company.
Bring a derivative action for breach of trust.
Petition the court for relief on grounds of unfairly prejudicial and oppressive conduct.
File a criminal complaint for theft.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 354(1) of CAMA 2020 allows a member to petition the court for relief on the grounds that the affairs of the company are being conducted in a manner that is oppressive or unfairly prejudicial to one or more members [12, 15].

quizzes.scenario: A minority shareholder in a small private company, Star Ltd, is unhappy with a one-time decision of the board to change the company's stationery supplier. He petitions for relief under Section 354.
options: The petition will likely succeed as it is a board decision.
The petition will likely fail because the conduct must usually be ongoing and not an isolated act.
The petition will succeed if he proves the new supplier is more expensive.
The petition must be brought by the company secretary.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: For a petition under Section 354 of CAMA 2020 to succeed, the oppressive or prejudicial conduct must typically be a course of conduct or an ongoing state of affairs, rather than a single isolated act [15].

quizzes.scenario: Apex Oil Plc intends to offer its shares to the general public for the first time to build a new refinery. Which regulatory body is the primary regulator responsible for overseeing this capital market operation?
options: Corporate Affairs Commission
Securities and Exchange Commission
Nigerian Exchange Limited
Federal High Court
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is established under Section 1 of the Investment and Securities Act (ISA) as the apex body responsible for overseeing capital market operations and regulating all investment and securities businesses in Nigeria [1-3].

quizzes.scenario: Zenith Energy Ltd wants to raise capital by issuing 200 million new shares to the public. In which market segment does this initial issuance and subscription take place?
options: Primary Market
Secondary Market
Money Market
Commodity Market
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The primary market is the segment where companies offer their securities to the public for the first time through an Initial Public Offer (IPO), and the company receives the money directly from subscribers [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: An investor named Tory, who bought 50,000 shares during Zenith Energy Ltd's IPO, now decides to sell them to another investor, Musa, through a stockbroker. Which market facilitates this transaction?
options: Primary Market
Secondary Market
Money Market
Insurance Market
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The secondary market is where investors buy and sell already-issued securities among themselves, providing liquidity and allowing shareholders to exit investments [4, 5].

quizzes.scenario: Dangote Cement Plc plans to issue bonds worth ₦150 billion. They hire Chapel Hill Denham to handle the structuring, documentation, and SEC filing. In what capacity is Chapel Hill Denham acting?
options: Underwriter
Stockbroker
Issuing House
Custodian
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Issuing Houses help companies raise long-term capital by structuring public offers, handling documentation, and managing SEC filings and allotment processes [6, 7].
