Charging a fee that is slightly higher than the average fee in his area
Arriving 10 minutes late for a court hearing
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Infamous conduct in a professional respect includes actions like commingling and misappropriating client's funds, which violate the RPC and LPA and destroy trust [3, 4].

quizzes.scenario: What is the primary function of the Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee (LPDC)?
options: To make rules of professional conduct
To consider and determine cases where it is alleged that a person has been guilty of professional misconduct in his capacity as a legal practitioner
To issue annual practicing licenses to lawyers
To determine who is qualified to be appointed as a Senior Advocate of Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 11 of the Legal Practitioners Act (LPA) establishes the LPDC with the primary function of considering and determining cases of alleged professional misconduct [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Who is the Chairman of the Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee (LPDC)?
options: The Chief Justice of Nigeria
A member of the Body of Benchers appointed by the Body
The President of the Court of Appeal
The Attorney-General of the Federation
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The LPDC is a committee of the Body of Benchers, and its Chairman is a Bencher appointed by the Body of Benchers [7, 8].

quizzes.scenario: If the LPDC finds a legal practitioner guilty of professional misconduct, which of the following directions can it legally make under the LPA?
options: It can direct that the practitioner's name be struck off the roll
It can sentence the practitioner to a term of imprisonment
It can order the police to seize the practitioner's office building
It can strip the practitioner of his university law degree
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 12(1) of the LPA provides that the LPDC can direct that the name be struck off the roll, suspend the practitioner, or admonish them [9, 10].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Dele is found guilty of professional misconduct by the LPDC, which directs that he be suspended from private practice for 3 years. Dele is highly dissatisfied and wants to appeal. To which court does the appeal lie?
options: The Court of Appeal
The Supreme Court of Nigeria
The Federal High Court
The General Council of the Bar
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Under Section 12(7) of the LPA, an appeal from any direction of the LPDC lies directly to the Supreme Court of Nigeria [11, 12].

quizzes.scenario: Within how many days must a legal practitioner file his appeal against a direction of the LPDC?
options: Within 14 days
Within 28 days
Within 90 days
Within 120 days
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 12(7) of the LPA provides that an appeal against a direction of the LPDC must be filed within 28 days from the date of the direction [13, 14].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer's name was struck off the roll in 2018 for financial dishonesty. In 2024, he wishes to apply for restoration of his name to the roll. To which body should he direct his application for restoration?
options: The Nigerian Bar Association National Executive Committee
The Body of Benchers
The Supreme Court of Nigeria
The Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The Body of Benchers has the statutory power to consider applications for the restoration of a legal practitioner's name to the roll after disciplinary action [15, 16].

quizzes.scenario: What is the legal status of a direction of the LPDC before the period for filing an appeal has expired?
options: It is immediately enforceable and cannot be stayed
It remains suspended and does not take effect until the time for appeal has elapsed or the appeal is determined
It is only a non-binding opinion
It must be published in the national newspapers immediately
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A direction of the LPDC does not take effect until the 28-day period for appeal has elapsed or, if an appeal is filed, until the appeal is finally determined or withdrawn [17, 18].

quizzes.scenario: Which of the following bodies is responsible for receiving petitions against lawyers and conducting preliminary investigations before forwarding them to the LPDC?
options: The Body of Benchers
The Legal Practitioners Disciplinary Committee itself
The Nigerian Bar Association (through its Disciplinary Committee)
The High Court Registry
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: The NBA, through its national or branch disciplinary committees, receives petitions, conducts preliminary investigations, and forwards prime facie cases to the LPDC [19, 20].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Funmi maintains a personal blog where she posts detailed and opinionated commentary on ongoing high-profile trials in Nigeria, arguing that several judges are receiving bribes from political parties. What is the ethical status of her blog?
options: It is a proper exercise of her freedom of expression and academic freedom.
It is professional misconduct as it constitutes a breach of the rule against trial publicity and brings the profession into disrepute.
It is proper provided she does not use her real name.
It is proper if she is not representing any of the parties in the trials she discusses.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 33 of the RPC on trial publicity prohibits lawyers from making extra-judicial statements that are likely to prejudice a fair trial, and Rule 39(2)(b) prohibits promotions that bring the profession into disrepute [1, 2].

quizzes.scenario: Mrs. Zainab, a legal practitioner, wants to host a weekly podcast on Instagram where she will discuss general legal issues like consumer rights, tenancy, and employment law. She will not discuss active court cases or offer advice on individual disputes. Is this permissible?
options: No
all social media activity by lawyers is strictly prohibited.
Yes
this is a permitted form of public legal education
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Yes

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Segun pays a social media influencer with 1 million followers on Twitter to post a tweet: "If you need the best and cheapest lawyer for your land or business registrations, DM Barrister Segun now!"
options: This is proper modern digital marketing.
This is professional misconduct as it involves soliciting professional employment through touts or public advertisements.
This is proper as long as the influencer has a valid business license.
This is proper provided Segun's fees do not fall below LPRO scales.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(3)(a) of the RPC explicitly prohibits a lawyer from soliciting professional employment, either directly or indirectly, through the use of touts, advertisements, or public media promotions [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: A lawyer, Miss Toyin, posts a video of herself in full legal robes dancing on TikTok to celebrate a recent court victory, with the caption "We won! Another major victory in court today!"
options: This is proper professional celebration.
This is improper as it is self-aggrandizing and brings the profession into disrepute under the RPC.
This is proper if she does not disclose the name of the client.
This is only improper if she was not actually called to the bar.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(2)(b), (d) and 39(3)(e) of the RPC 2023 prohibit advertising that brings the profession into disrepute, includes claims about success, or involves self-aggrandizement [1, 7].

quizzes.scenario: A law firm, "Justice League Partners," creates a professional LinkedIn page listing their practice areas, office address, and the publications of their partners.
options: This is a prohibited form of digital advertising.
This is a permitted and proper form of informative data publication.
This is only permitted if the firm pays a social media fee to the NBA.
This is improper because law firms are not allowed to have social media pages.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 39(4) of the RPC 2023 permits a lawyer to publish informative data about himself or his firm, including names, addresses, and academic qualifications, on directories and professional platforms [8, 9].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Chidi, an experienced legal practitioner, decides to form a partnership with Mr. John, a qualified and licensed Chartered Accountant, to offer a "one-stop shop" for legal and tax services. Is this partnership valid under the RPC?
options: Yes
multi-disciplinary partnerships are allowed under modern business laws.
No
a lawyer shall not form a partnership with a non-lawyer if any of the activities of the partnership consists of the practice of law.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: provided the legal and accounting services are kept in separate rooms.

quizzes.scenario: A legal practitioner, Mr. Sola, wants to employ a qualified and licensed foreign lawyer as an associate in his Lagos law firm to handle international commercial agreements. Is this permissible?
options: Yes
there are no restrictions on employing foreign associates.
Only with the express approval and consent of the Attorney-General of the Federation (AGF) or under reciprocity rules.
Yes
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: No

quizzes.scenario: A multinational auditing firm, "ABC Accounting," hires Barrister Kemi as a salaried legal officer. Her duties include drafting contracts and representing ABC's corporate clients in tax disputes before the High Court. What is the ethical status of Kemi's work?
options: It is proper as she is a qualified and called legal practitioner.
It is improper as she is aiding a non-lawyer (the accounting firm) in the unauthorized practice of law and appearing as an advocate for an employer.
It is proper if the corporate clients sign a waiver.
It is only proper if the accounting firm pays her practicing fees.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Rule 3(1) of the RPC prohibits a lawyer from aiding a non-lawyer in the unauthorized practice of law, and Rule 8(1) prohibits a salaried employee from appearing as an advocate in court for his employer [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: Barrister Tunde is representing a client in a land transaction. He agrees to split his professional fees with a local estate agent who helped negotiate the price. Is this ethical?
options: Yes
fee-splitting is a standard real estate practice.
No
a lawyer shall not share his legal fees with a non-lawyer
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Yes

quizzes.scenario: A law firm, "Apex Chambers," wants to hire a registered medical doctor as a full-time consultant to help analyze medical reports in personal injury cases.
options: This is proper
provided the doctor does not practice law or appear as an advocate.
This is a prohibited form of partnership with a non-lawyer.
This is only proper if the doctor has a law degree.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: A

quizzes.scenario: A British barrister, Mr. Arthur, is hired by a major oil company to represent them in an international commercial arbitration held in Lagos, Nigeria. The arbitration is governed by Nigerian law. Can Mr. Arthur appear as counsel?
options: No
only lawyers called to the Nigerian Bar can appear in any legal proceeding in Nigeria.
Yes
a foreign lawyer can act as counsel in arbitration proceedings in Nigeria
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Yes

quizzes.scenario: An American attorney, Miss Sarah, wants to open a branch of her New York law firm in Abuja to advise clients on New York corporate law. Sarah has not been called to the Nigerian Bar.
options: Sarah can open the office since she is only advising on New York law.
Sarah cannot open or practice law in Nigeria without being called to the Nigerian Bar
as private practice is restricted to enrolled practitioners.
Sarah can open the office if she partners with a Nigerian lawyer who owns 51% of the firm.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: B

quizzes.scenario: Under what statutory framework can a foreign lawyer be admitted to practice law in Nigeria for a specific transaction or case?
options: By applying for a temporary work visa from the Nigerian Immigration Service
By obtaining a special warrant from the Chief Justice of Nigeria under Section 2(2) of the LPA
By paying a registration fee to the General Council of the Bar
By obtaining a recommendation from the President of the Court of Appeal
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 2(2) of the LPA allows the CJN, if satisfied that it is expedient to do so, to issue a warrant to a foreign legal practitioner to practice for the purpose of a particular proceeding [5, 6].

quizzes.scenario: A Nigerian lawyer, Mr. Wale, forms a partnership with a French law firm, "Paris Juris LLP," to offer cross-border legal services. Wale resides in Lagos and manages the Lagos office, while the French partners manage the Paris office. Is this permissible?
options: No
international partnerships are strictly prohibited.
Yes
provided the partnership is conducted in accordance with the LPA and rules on partnership
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Yes

quizzes.scenario: When a foreign legal practitioner is granted a special warrant by the Chief Justice of Nigeria to represent a client in a specific case, what is the scope of his right to practice?
options: He can practice in all courts and handle any case for a period of one year.
His right to practice is strictly limited to the specific proceedings or case for which the warrant was granted.
He can only act as a solicitor but cannot appear in court as an advocate.
He can only practice if he is accompanied by the Attorney-General of the Federation.
quizzes.verbatimCorrection: Section 2(2) of the LPA limits the warrant to the specific proceedings and any appeal arising therefrom, meaning it is not a general license to practice [6, 9].
